skill.name: scikit-bio
skill.description: Use scikit-bio guidance for biological sequence analysis, phylogenetic trees, diversity metrics, ordination, and microbiome statistics.
skill.summary: Analyze biological sequences, trees, diversity metrics, and microbiome data with scikit-bio.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review shows they are false positives from Markdown code fences, biological terminology, and PCoA labels such as PC2. The only real risk indicators are a standard package installation command and links to official documentation, GitHub, and forum resources.

low_findings.title: Benign Package Installation Command
low_findings.description: The skill includes a shell example to install scikit-bio with uv pip install. This is a normal setup step, but it may download packages from package indexes if a user chooses to run it.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is visible in an installation section and has a fixed package name. There is no evidence of obfuscation, dynamic command construction, or unauthorized execution.

low_findings.title: External Resource Links
low_findings.description: The skill links to scikit-bio documentation, the scikit-bio GitHub repository, and the QIIME 2 forum. These are expected reference links, not exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are presented as additional resources and point to recognizable project documentation and support locations. No code sends data to these URLs.

low_findings.title: Static Pattern Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The external command, C2 keyword, weak crypto, and reconnaissance alerts resolve to Markdown fences, biological API examples, PCoA coordinate labels, and performance notes. No malicious intent or executable payload was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines show documentation examples and scientific terms, not shell execution, C2 behavior, cryptography, or reconnaissance. The pattern names do not match the semantic context.

content.value_statement: Bioinformatics work often spans sequences, trees, distance matrices, and microbial ecology statistics. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical scikit-bio guidance for those workflows.
actual_capabilities: Guides DNA, RNA, and protein sequence manipulation with scikit-bio classes.
Explains pairwise alignment, multiple alignment handling, and consensus workflows.
Supports phylogenetic tree construction, traversal, pruning, rerooting, and distance calculations.
Covers alpha diversity, beta diversity, UniFrac, PERMANOVA, ANOSIM, and Mantel tests.
Shows ordination workflows including PCoA, CCA, RDA, and plotting-ready result interpretation.
Documents FASTA, FASTQ, Newick, BIOM, and distance matrix input and output patterns.
limitations: It provides guidance and examples, not a bundled scikit-bio runtime.
Users must supply valid biological data files and compatible Python dependencies.
Large sequence, BIOM, or tree analyses may need memory planning and batching.
Statistical conclusions still require domain review and appropriate experimental design.

use_cases.title: Compare Microbiome Communities
use_cases.description: Calculate diversity metrics, run PERMANOVA, and prepare ordination summaries for sample groups.

use_cases.title: Build Sequence Analysis Workflows
use_cases.description: Read biological sequence files, transform sequences, find motifs, and prepare FASTA outputs.

use_cases.title: Teach Biological Data Methods
use_cases.description: Create clear examples for alignments, trees, distance matrices, and ecological statistics.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize a Basic Workflow
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner user planning a first scikit-bio task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how to use scikit-bio to read DNA sequences, reverse complement them, and write a FASTA output.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Diversity Analysis
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate microbiome analysis with sample metadata.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a scikit-bio workflow for alpha diversity, beta diversity, PCoA, and PERMANOVA using a count table and group labels.

prompt_templates.title: Review Phylogenetic Methods
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced analysis involving trees and distance matrices.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare neighbor joining, UPGMA, and phylogenetic diversity calculations in scikit-bio for my sequence dataset.

prompt_templates.title: Debug an Analysis Pipeline
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced troubleshooting for broken bioinformatics scripts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my scikit-bio workflow conceptually and identify likely causes of mismatched IDs, invalid counts, or ordination errors.

output_examples.input: A user asks how to compare microbial communities across treatment groups.
output: A recommended workflow covering table validation, alpha diversity, Bray-Curtis or UniFrac distances, PCoA, and PERMANOVA.
A checklist of required inputs, including count matrix, sample IDs, metadata, and optional phylogenetic tree.
Interpretation notes for ordination axes, effect size, p-values, and multiple testing concerns.

output_examples.input: A user asks why a scikit-bio tree workflow fails after pruning taxa.
output: A focused diagnosis of ID mismatches between the tree, feature table, and distance matrix.
Suggested validation steps for tip names, missing taxa, rootedness, and branch lengths.
A concise recovery plan that preserves biological meaning before rerunning metrics.

best_practices: Validate sequence IDs, sample IDs, and feature IDs before combining files.
Use generators or chunked workflows for large sequence files and BIOM tables.
Choose diversity metrics and statistical tests that match the study design.
anti_patterns: Do not treat relative abundance values as integer count tables for count-based metrics.
Do not compare UniFrac distances without a matching phylogenetic tree and feature IDs.
Do not interpret ordination plots without checking variance explained and metadata quality.

faq.question: Does this skill install scikit-bio for me?
faq.answer: No. It explains installation and usage patterns, but the user must manage the Python environment.

faq.question: Can it help with microbiome diversity analysis?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers alpha diversity, beta diversity, UniFrac, PCoA, PERMANOVA, ANOSIM, and related workflows.

faq.question: Does it support phylogenetic trees?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes tree reading, construction, traversal, rerooting, pruning, and distance calculations.

faq.question: Can it read FASTQ and FASTA files?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents scikit-bio patterns for FASTA, FASTQ, GenBank, Newick, BIOM, and distance matrices.

faq.question: Is this only for microbiome projects?
faq.answer: No. It also applies to sequence manipulation, alignments, phylogenetics, ordination, and protein embedding analysis.

faq.question: What should users prepare before asking for help?
faq.answer: Users should describe file formats, sample IDs, feature IDs, metadata columns, and the biological question.
